---
title: "Datasets"
subtitle: "Use fmrimod.dataset as the substrate for modeling, contrasts, and group analysis."
jupyter: python3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

## Dataset API Overview

`fmrimod.dataset` is the canonical Python home for dataset containers and
storage adapters. The compatibility package `fmridataset` is only a re-export
facade for migrated code; new examples should import from `fmrimod` or
`fmrimod.dataset`.

The dataset layer is intentionally a substrate for modeling rather than a
separate project. A typical workflow is:

```text
matrix_dataset / fmri_dataset
  -> fmri_lm or single-trial estimator
  -> contrasts
  -> group_data or group_fit
```

Use these entry points as the public surface:

| Need | Use | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Timing grid | `SamplingFrame` | Shared by design and dataset code. |
| In-memory matrices | `matrix_dataset()` | Tests, simulations, and pre-extracted features. |
| Image-backed data | `fmri_dataset()` | Real image IO should enter through the neuroim-compatible boundary. |
| Low-rank features | `latent_dataset()` | Stores component scores with optional loadings. |
| Study tables | `study_dataset()` / `fmri_group()` | Subject-level containers feeding native group analysis. |
| Chunked voxel work | `data_chunks()` / `voxel_index_chunks()` | Batch execution without changing the model contract. |
| BIDS-HDF5 archives | `bids_h5_dataset()` / `compress_bids_study()` | Optional HDF5/BIDS IO boundary, not a second tutorial track. |

## Sampling Frames

`SamplingFrame` describes the acquired time grid, including run lengths. In
the canonical dataset contract, `samples` are acquisition times in seconds.

::: {#d3929796 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import fmrimod as fm

sf = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=[50, 50], TR=2.0)
sf.samples[:6], sf.blocklens
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
(array([ 1.,  3.,  5.,  7.,  9., 11.]), array([50, 50]))
```
:::
:::


## Matrix Data

Use `matrix_dataset()` when data already live in memory as a time by voxel
matrix. Real image-backed workflows should route through the dataset adapters
and, ultimately, neuroim-python containers at the image boundary.

::: {#9d60298d .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
rng = np.random.default_rng(11)
events = pd.DataFrame({
    "run": [1, 1, 2, 2],
    "onset": [10, 30, 12, 32],
    "condition": ["face", "scene", "face", "scene"],
})

data = rng.normal(size=(100, 20))
dset = fm.matrix_dataset(
    data,
    tr=2.0,
    run_length=[50, 50],
    event_table=events,
)

dset
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2}
```
FmriDataset(n_runs=2, n_timepoints=100, n_voxels=20)
```
:::
:::


::: {#ec538126 .cell execution_count=3}
``` {.python .cell-code}
dset.n_runs, dset.n_timepoints, dset.n_voxels
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3}
```
(2, 100, 20)
```
:::
:::


::: {#35df23ab .cell execution_count=4}
``` {.python .cell-code}
dset.get_data(rows=[0, 1, 2], cols=[0, 1]).shape, dset.get_all_data().shape
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4}
```
((3, 2), (100, 20))
```
:::
:::


## Image and Archive IO

`fmri_dataset()` is the preferred constructor when a workflow starts from
image-like objects or paths. It keeps image IO at the boundary and returns the
same typed `FmriDataset` object used by matrix-backed examples.

BIDS-HDF5 helpers are available when a workflow needs a compact archive:

- `compress_bids_study()` writes a v1 BIDS-HDF5 archive from already
  materialized datasets, or from an optional BIDS-directory IO path when the
  BIDS/NIfTI dependencies are installed.
- `bids_h5_dataset()` opens the archive as a study object whose scan backends
  follow the same feature-space storage contract as matrix, latent, HDF5, and
  NIfTI-backed datasets.

These helpers are documented here because they are dataset substrate. There is
no separate backend-registry or BIDS-HDF5 tutorial to keep in sync.

## Latent Data

Latent datasets store component scores and optional loadings. They can be used
when the time series has been compressed before modeling.

::: {#6ea9d8de .cell execution_count=5}
``` {.python .cell-code}
scores = rng.normal(size=(100, 4))
loadings = rng.normal(size=(4, 20))

latent = fm.latent_dataset(
    scores,
    loadings=loadings,
    tr=2.0,
    run_length=[50, 50],
    event_table=events,
)

latent.n_runs, latent.n_voxels, latent.get_scores(0).shape
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5}
```
(2, 20, (50, 4))
```
:::
:::


## Chunking

Chunk helpers make it easy to split voxel-space work into reviewable batches.
`data_chunks()` yields typed `DataChunk` objects carrying data and indices;
`voxel_index_chunks()` is the legacy index-only helper.

::: {#80acfdba .cell execution_count=6}
``` {.python .cell-code}
chunks = fm.data_chunks(dset, nchunks=4)
[(chunk.chunk_num, chunk.data.shape, chunk.voxel_ind.tolist()) for chunk in chunks]
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6}
```
[(1, (100, 5), [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]),
 (2, (100, 5), [5, 6, 7, 8, 9]),
 (3, (100, 5), [10, 11, 12, 13, 14]),
 (4, (100, 5), [15, 16, 17, 18, 19])]
```
:::
:::


## Migrating From fmridataset

Existing `fmridataset` imports should move mechanically to `fmrimod.dataset`
or the root `fmrimod` namespace. During consolidation, `fmridataset` re-exports
only implemented canonical objects identity-equivalently:

::: {#228a9ed5 .cell execution_count=7}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import fmridataset
import fmrimod.dataset as ds

fmridataset.FmriDataset is ds.FmriDataset
```
:::


Names that have not landed in `fmrimod.dataset` are intentionally absent from
the facade rather than implemented as placeholders.
